tools.name: app_control_start
tools.description: Start (launch or focus) the target application. Optionally pass command-line arguments. Begins an app-control session targeting this app.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

args.description: Optional command-line arguments to launch the app with

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you are starting this app

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_observe
tools.description: Capture the current window state of the target application — returns lifecycle state (running/missing/minimized), an optional screenshot, and window bounds. Use this before issuing input actions, or to check progress without acting.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

settle_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait before capturing so the target app and the WindowServer can flush pending input + composite a fresh frame. Default ~200ms (sized for emulator-class apps at 60fps). Pass 0 for static UIs; raise for slow-feedback apps.

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_press
tools.description: Press a single key in the target application, with optional modifiers (cmd/shift/option/ctrl) and a hold duration in milliseconds.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

modifiers.description: Modifier list, e.g. ["cmd", "shift"]. Omit for no modifiers.

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you are pressing this key

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_combo
tools.description: Press multiple keys simultaneously in the target application (e.g. cmd+shift+4). Use for keyboard shortcuts where every key is held at once.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

keys.description: Sequence of keys pressed simultaneously, e.g. ["cmd", "shift", "4"]

reasoning.description: Explanation of why you are pressing this combo

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_sequence
tools.description: Send an ordered batch of single-key presses to the target application. The target app is activated once at the start, then each step is pressed serially with optional inter-step gaps. PREFER this over multiple back-to-back app_control_press calls when sending an ordered batch (e.g. menu navigation, repeated movement) — sequence runs in a single round-trip with no window for keyboard focus to drift between presses.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

steps.description: Ordered list of single-key presses to execute serially.

modifiers.description: Modifier list for this step, e.g. ["cmd", "shift"]. Omit for no modifiers.

duration_ms.description: Hold duration for this key in milliseconds (default 50)

gap_ms.description: Pause after this step before starting the next, in milliseconds (default 30)

reasoning.description: Explanation of what this sequence does and why

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_type
tools.description: Type literal text into the target application at the current focus. Ensure the intended field is focused before calling.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you are typing and why

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_click
tools.description: Click at the given (x, y) coordinates inside the target application's window. Defaults to a single left-click; pass `button` and/or `double` to vary.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you see and why you are clicking here

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_drag
tools.description: Drag from (from_x, from_y) to (to_x, to_y) inside the target application's window. Defaults to left button.

app.description: Bundle ID (preferred, e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') or process name of the target application

reasoning.description: Explanation of what you are dragging and why

target_client_id.description: ID of the specific client to target. Required when multiple clients support host_app_control; omit when only one is connected. Obtain IDs from `assistant clients list --capability host_app_control`.

tools.name: app_control_stop
tools.description: Stop the current app-control session. When `app` is omitted, stops whichever app currently holds the session. This is the terminal action for an app-control flow.

app.description: Optional bundle ID or process name. When omitted, stops whichever app currently holds the session.
